SAMUEL SIEGEL
SCOTT NICHOLS
LAW OFFICES
Srr,cel AND NlcHot-s
A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION
3610 LONG BEACH BOULEVARD
SECOND FLOOR
LONG BEACH. CALIFORNIA 9O8O7
TELEPHONES:
(2r3) 595-OO72

TELECOPI ER
t2r3) 595-4332
d,

c.Y'
December 2, L991
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814
Attention: Mr. Jeevan Ahuja
Dear Mr. Ahuja:
As the City Attorney for the City of Pico Rivera, I respectfully
request confirmation in writing of the opinion rendered to me by
you in our telephone conversation of November 25 , l-991-. The
opinion pertains to Rick L. Mercado, Sr., a member of the City
Council of the City of Pico Rivera. As you will recall, the wife
of Mr. Mercado serves as a member of the Board of Directors of the
Pico Water District, 
a water purveyor which supplies water to
approximately one-half of the City of Pico Rivera.
The City of Pico Rivera has entered into a coalition with
surrounding cities served by the Central Water Basin in an effort
to implement a strategy to contain contaminated ground water in the
San Gabriel Valley. 
Along with the aforesaid cities, 
several
basin municipal water districts are also members of this coalition.
It is anticipated that this coalition will ultimately be formed
into a Joint Powers Authority. 
The City Council desires to have
Councilman Mercado represent the City on this coalition/Joint
Powers Authority.
The Pico Water District, of which Mrs. Mercado is a Director, is
also a public agency. As such, she is paid a specific amount for
every meeting of the Water District which she attends.
As I understood it, the aforesaid informal opinion which you have
rendered was in essence that any conflict of interest would be most
remote. You further stated that if such a remote conflict did in
fact 
arise, 
Councilman Mercado would merely abstain from
participating in the subject action. 
Such an unlikely conflict
would involve financial benefit to the Agency which Mrs. Mercado
represents. However, ds you indicated, since the Agency serves
more than 502 of the City, it is reasonably foreseeable that the
decision will not have a material financial effect di-stinguishable

sections.question_synthetic: The City Attorney of Pico Rivera seeks written confirmation of a prior informal FPPC opinion regarding whether City Councilman Rick L. Mercado has a conflict of interest in representing the city on a coalition/Joint Powers Authority to address contaminated groundwater, given that his wife serves as a Director of the Pico Water District, a member of the coalition.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter (not an FPPC response). No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections exist. The substantive legal inquiry is embedded in the body of the letter. The document references a prior informal telephone opinion from November 25, 1991.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: The City Attorney of Pico Rivera seeks written confirmation of a prior informal FPPC opinion regarding whether City Councilman Rick L. Mercado has a conflict of interest in representing the city on a coalition/Joint Powers Authority to address contaminated groundwater, given that his wife serves as a Director of the Pico Water District, a member of the coalition.

CALIFORNIA 9O8O7 TELEPHONES: (2r3) 595-OO72 {2r3) s95-O149 TELECOPI ER t2r3) 595-4332 d, 'TI <., c.Y' December 2, L991 Fair Political Practices Commission 428 J Street, Suite 800 Sacramento, CA 95814 Attention: Mr. Jeevan Ahuja Dear Mr. Ahuja: As the City Attorney for the City of Pico Rivera, I respectfully request confirmation in writing of the opinion rendered to me by you in our telephone conversation of November 25 , l-991-. The opinion pertains to Rick L. Mercado, Sr., a member of the City Council of the City of Pico Rivera. As you will recall, the wife of Mr. Mercado serves as a member of the Board of Directors of the Pico Water District, a water purveyor which supplies water to approximately one-half of the City of Pico Rivera. The City of Pico Rivera has entered into a coalition with surrounding cities served by the Central Water Basin in an effort to implement a strategy to contain contaminated ground water in the San Gabriel Valley. Along with the aforesaid cities, several basin municipal water districts are also members of this coalition. It is anticipated that this coalition will ultimately be formed into a Joint Powers Authority. The City Council desires to have Councilman Mercado represent the City on this coalition/Joint Powers Authority. The Pico Water District, of which Mrs. Mercado is a Director, is also a public agency. As such, she is paid a specific amount for every meeting of the Water District which she attends. As I understood it, the aforesaid informal opinion which you have rendered was in essence that any conflict of interest would be most remote. You further stated that if such a remote conflict did in fact arise, Councilman Mercado would merely abstain from participating in the subject action. Such an unlikely conflict would involve financial benefit to the Agency which Mrs. Mercado represents. However, ds you indicated, since the Agency serves more than 502 of the City, it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will not have a material financial effect di-stinguishable ,t .r.r, (rS dl ts Fair PoIiticaI Practices commission December 2, l99L Page 2 distinguishable on its effect on the public generally. embedding.summary: The City Attorney of Pico Rivera requests a formal written FPPC opinion confirming an informal telephone opinion regarding potential conflict of interest for Councilman Mercado's participation in a groundwater contamination coalition, given his wife's position on the Pico Water District Board.
